title: Greenea tetrandra
taxonomy: Greenea tetrandra is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is classified in the genus Greenea, which is a group of tropical plants. The Rubiaceae family, also known as the madder family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within this family, Greenea tetrandra is one of many species that share similar characteristics and growth habits. Greenea tetrandra's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

Greenea tetrandra is also used to treat digestive issues and is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant's leaves are used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses, but preliminary studies suggest the plant's potential in treating various health issues.

intro: Greenea tetrandra, also known as Kala bel, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Greenea tetrandra include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
